title: Do not rely solely on color for status indicators
summary: AI-generated status systems universally use the traffic-light color mapping (green=success, yellow=pending, red=error, gray=inactive) with pill-shaped badges and uppercase text, without accessible alternatives.
instruction: Use text labels, icons, or patterns alongside color to communicate status. Ensure status is distinguishable in grayscale and for users with color vision deficiencies. Reserve the green/red binary for time-critical signals only.
rationale: The traffic-light color mapping fails for the ~8% of users with red-green colorblindness and varies in cultural meaning across markets. When color is the only differentiator, the affordance breaks for screen reader users and anyone viewing in high-contrast or grayscale mode.
violations: Green/red/yellow dot or pill as the only status differentiator
Pill/rounded-full shape for all badges regardless of platform shape language
Uppercase or semibold text within the badge reducing readability
No additional indicator (icon, pattern, text) alongside the color dot
Default color mapping: green=active, yellow=pending/warning, red=error, gray=inactive
controls: Icon + text + color combined for status communication
Color with sufficient non-color differentiator (shape, position, pattern)
Status communicated through layout or group position instead of per-item badges
borderlineCases: System monitoring dashboards where second-resolution status recognition is critical
contextualExceptions: Deployment pipelines where red/yellow/green is an industry standard convention
detectorSignal: Small pill or dot-shaped elements using the colors green, red, yellow, or gray adjacent to text labels describing status.
matcherProposal: Detect small rounded elements (pill or dot) colored with status-typical colors (green, red, amber, gray) positioned next to text labels or in table cells.
detectorLimitations: Cannot determine if sufficient non-color differentiation exists alongside the color indicator
falsePositiveScenarios: Status badges that already include icons or text alongside color
mitigationStrategy: Flag as an error when color-only indicators are detected without accompanying text or icon differentiation.

fixturePlans.violation: Status table column with green dot (active), gray dot (offline), red dot (error), no text labels, no icons.
fixturePlans.control: Status with colored dot + descriptive icon + text label (checkmark+Active, cross+Error, clock+Pending).
fixturePlans.borderline: CI pipeline with red/yellow/green convention where the labels are already descriptive.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
overlapNotes: Related to a11y.visible-focus in the broader a11y category but focuses on color-only status indicators.
